summary:
Generalized skin peeling occurred months after starting antiepileptics; this suggests a fixed drug eruption as opposed to more serious conditions such as Stevens-Johnson syndrome/toxic epidermal necrolysis based on limited mucocutaneous involvement and specific temporal association with treatment start. Agent2 reasoned that since there was no extensive damage involving mucus membranes nor significant system-wide effects typical for severe reactions like Stevens Johnson’s disease/Toxic Epidermal Necrosis but instead just patchy skin changes confined mostly outside hands & feet – it pointed towards Fixed Drug Eruption being highly plausible cause here linked directly back to recent introduction of new meds including those used against seizures related from Neuro Cisticercosisis condition previously present within brain tissue itself before current management plan began using these drugs again now causing side effect noted by patient themselves reporting same issue shortly thereafter upon resuming said therapy regimen aimed at controlling seizure activity associated originally stemming out neurological parasite infection known medically termed 'NeuroCist' . 

Key Points Summarised:

* Generalized skin peeling appeared approximately monthly after beginning an anti-seizure medicine
* No signs pointing toward life-threatening allergic events like Stevens-Johnson syndrome or toxic epidermal necrolysis were observed
* Diagnosis leans heavily into "fixed-drug eruptions" because only superficial patches visible primarily where not protected areas i.e., face etc...
